title: Molten Pungent Kenyan Eggs Frittata
description: A delectable Kenyan take on a classic Italian frittata, featuring the pungency of kachumbari and the richness of eggs. Serve with a side of kachumbari for an authentic experience.
instructions: Beat the eggs in a large bowl and set aside.
Heat the ghee in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the onion, habanero, and garlic and saut2e until the onion is translucent.
Add the dried fish, cumin powder, coriander powder, salt, and black pepper and stir to combine.
Add the kale and stir to wilt.
Pour the eggs over the mixture and cook until the edges start to set.
Place the skillet under the broiler for 2-3 minutes or until the eggs are molten and the frittata is golden brown.
Remove from the oven and let cool slightly before slicing and serving.
Serve hot, garnished with kachumbari if desired.
Remove any excess fat from the skillet before serving.
